21 May 2026
PINEAPPLE POWER CORPORATION PLC
Results of General Meeting and Capital Reorganisation
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC (LSE: PNPL) (the "
Company
"), a special purpose acquisition company, announces that at its General Meeting held earlier today, the resolution proposed, as referred to in the Company's announcement of 1 May 2026, was duly passed.
Proxy votes received appointing the Chair were as follows:
No
Resolution1,2

SPECIAL RESOLUTION

Sub-division of share capital and amendments to articles of association

1. The full text of the resolution can be found in the Notice of the General Meeting sent to shareholders on the Company's website at
https://www.pineapple-powercorp.com
, and in the National Storage Mechanism of the FCA at
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
.
2. As at the record date for eligibility to vote at the Company's General Meeting, the Company had 83,332,877 Ordinary Shares in issue. .
3. Votes 'Withheld' are not a vote in law and have not been counted in the calculation of the votes % 'For' and % votes 'Against' the resolutions.
Accordingly, at the Record Time for the sub-division (being 6.00pm on today's date), the capital reorganisation will become effective and each existing ordinary share of £0.01 (
Existing Ordinary Shares
) will be sub-divided into one new ordinary share of £0.001 (
New Ordinary Shares
) and one deferred share of £0.009. Applications have been made for the New Ordinary Shares to be admitted to the Official List of the FCA and to trading on the London Stock Exchange's Main Market (
Admission
), and dealings in the New Ordinary Shares are expected to commence at 8.00 am on 22 May 2026.
The ticker and ISIN for the New Ordinary Shares will remain the same as for the Existing Ordinary Shares, as set out below:
·
Ticker: PNPL
·
ISIN: GB00BD0SN947
Following Admission, the total number of New Ordinary Shares in issue will be 83,332,877, all with voting rights. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company's share capital pursuant to the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
- END -

latest.title: Notice of GM
latest.content:
1 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6766C
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC
01 May 2026
1 May 2026
PINEAPPLE POWER CORPORATION PLC
('the Company')
Notice of General Meeting
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC (LSE: PNPL), a special purpose acquisition company, is publishing a Circular to be sent to shareholders today, containing a Notice convening a General Meeting of the Company to be held at the offices of Fladgate LLP, 16 Great Queen Street, London WC2B 5DG at 11.00 a.m. on 21 May 2026. Copies of the Circular containing the Notice of General Meeting and a Form of Proxy have been posted to shareholders that have elected to receive documentation in hard copy format and are also available on the Company's website at https://www.pineapple-powercorp.com/news/, and a copy will be added to the National Storage Mechanism of the FCA at:-
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
.
At the General Meeting, the Company will propose a capital reorganisation that will consist of a sub-division by which each Existing Ordinary Share will be sub-divided into one New Ordinary Share of £0.001 each and one new Deferred Share of £0.009 each. The proposed Sub-Division necessitates an alteration to the Company's Articles to include the rights and restrictions attached to the Deferred Shares, and so changes to the Articles of the Company are proposed.
The timetable of events is:
Publication and posting of this Circular: 1 May 2026.
Latest time and date for lodging Forms of Proxy or for submitting proxy instructions via the CREST electronic proxy appointment service: 11.00 a.m. on 19 May 2026.
Record time for those Shareholders on the register of members entitled to attend or vote at the General Meeting: 11.00 a.m. 19 May 2026.
Time and date of the General Meeting: 11.00 a.m. on 21 May 2026.
Announcement of the result of the General Meeting: 21 May 2026.
Record Date: 6.00 p.m. on 21 May 2026.
Effective time of the Sub-Division, issue of New Ordinary Shares and admission of the New Ordinary Shares: 8.00 a.m. on 22 May 2026.
- END -
ENQUIRIES

latest.title: Annual Financial Report Delay
latest.content:
Today 18:00
RNS Number : 7188B
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC
23 April 2026
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC
Annual Financial Report Delay
(the "Company")
April 23, 2026
Delay in Publication of the Annual Financial Report for the Year Ended 31 December 2025
Pineapple Power Corporation plc (LSE: PNPL)
, a special purpose acquisition company, announces a delay in the publication of its audited annual financial report for the year ended 31 December 2025 (the
"Annual Report"
), which was due to be released by 30 April 2026.
The delay has arisen as the Board and its advisors work to conclude certain matters required to stabilise and strengthen the Company's financial position. These discussions are ongoing and are a priority for the Board to ensure the "going concern" basis of the financial statements is robustly supported.
A further update regarding the expected publication date will be provided in due course.

latest.title: Directorate change
latest.content:
19 Feb 2026 18:15
RNS Number : 6748T
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC
19 February 2026
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC
Board Changes
(the "Company")
The Board of Pineapple Power Corporation PLC (LSE: PNPL) announces with great sadness that Andrew Holland, Non
‑
Executive Director of the Company, passed away on 16 January 2026. Mr Holland served on the Board since May 2017, contributing nearly nine years of dedicated and valued service. The Board extends its sincere condolences to Mr Holland's family and expresses its gratitude for his significant contribution to the Company throughout his tenure.
The Company announces that Mr Adam Colton Park has been appointed as an Independent Non
‑
Executive Director with effect from 10 February 2026. Mr Park brings extensive experience as a non
‑
executive director and chief operating officer across several organisations, with particular expertise in corporate governance, compliance, transaction management and strategic oversight.
Mr Park served as a Non Executive Director of AMTE Power plc having been appointed on 27 October 2016. The company entered administration on 19 January 2024.
Chairman, Claudio Morandi, commented:
"On behalf of the Board, I would like to express our profound sadness at the passing of Andrew. His guidance and support were greatly valued, and our thoughts are with his family at this difficult time.
We are also pleased to welcome Adam to the Board. His extensive experience as a COO and Non-Executive Director, combined with a strong track record in leading organisations through growth, strategic change and complex transitions, will bring significant insight to Pineapple Power. His pragmatic, governance-focused approach, along with his ability to work effectively with investors and leadership teams, makes him an excellent addition at this stage of the Company's development."
Save as disclosed above, there is no further information to be disclosed pursuant to UKLR 6.4.8R(2) to UKLR 6.4.8R(6).

company_30s.what_they_do: Pineapple Power Corporation is a cash‑shell company that is looking to merge with or acquire another business to create a new operating entity. It has not yet launched any products or generated revenue.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company is positioned to become a platform for a reverse takeover (RTO) in the technology/health‑tech space. If it successfully completes an RTO, the new business could tap into growing markets for medical AI solutions, which are projected to expand into multi‑billion‑pound markets over the next decade.
company_30s.current_state: PNPL is currently in a pre‑launch phase. It has been suspended and recently restored to the market. The company has no operating history, no revenue, and is still seeking a suitable takeover target. The share price is effectively a placeholder until the company completes its RTO or finds another catalyst.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show routine corporate governance updates: a change of directors, a notice of a general meeting, and a delayed annual report. These are standard procedural items for a shell company.

Two key themes emerge: (1) the company is actively working to secure a reverse takeover partner, evidenced by a recent RTO announcement with a Hamburg‑based AI firm; (2) it is maintaining its listing status and complying with regulatory requirements, as shown by the restoration of trading and the AGM results.
These actions suggest the company is trying to stay afloat and keep its options open, but no concrete business progress or funding has been announced.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Murmurs of a potential RTO, but largely silent overall.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders hope the upcoming RTO will unlock value and that the company will eventually list a profitable tech business.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others view the shell as a hollow vehicle with no clear path to profitability and fear it may be delisted again or fail to find a suitable partner.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been flat for months; recent chatter is limited to speculation about the RTO, indicating no momentum building.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker are flat, meaning there is no current public interest. This is a classic contrarian signal – low noise, high surprise potential.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of a reverse takeover completion with the Hamburg AI firm – the first step toward a tangible business.
Any disclosure of new funding or a capital raise that would give the shell cash to pursue the takeover.
A scheduled earnings or financial statement release that shows a positive cash flow or a clear path to profitability.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a spike in daily trading volume accompanied by a break above the recent 20‑day moving average. Also monitor any changes in the board that signal a new strategic direction.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early stages of a potential crash‑recovery cycle – it has not yet shown a clear bottom. The APEX compression score is low, so the market has not yet tightened its expectations. A mean‑reversion move would likely come after the company delivers a substantive announcement, such as a completed RTO or a funding round.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – no concrete signal color, but contrarian factors suggest a watchful stance.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the shell successfully completes the RTO, the new business could be valued at several times the current share price. The mean‑reversion thesis would be that the market price is below the intrinsic value of the combined entity, offering a potential upside of 200‑300% if the takeover proceeds smoothly.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company may fail to find a takeover partner, face regulatory delays, or be delisted again. The worst‑case scenario is a total loss of the invested capital if the share price falls to zero.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 200‑300% if the RTO is successful. Potential downside: 100% if the share becomes worthless. The asymmetry is roughly 2:1, but the probability of the upside is low without a clear catalyst.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only after a confirmed RTO announcement or a significant funding event. Use a small position (e.g., 2‑3% of portfolio) and set a stop 5% below the announcement price to protect against a failed takeover.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume surge of at least 20% over the 3‑month average and a close above the 20‑day moving average. These are the earliest signs of a potential bounce.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted or files for bankruptcy, exit immediately. No other terminal risks are evident at present.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Pineapple Power is a shell with no business yet; its contrarian appeal hinges on a successful reverse takeover, but until that catalyst appears the risk‑reward balance remains highly uncertain.
